title: Keep the build method pure and cheap
description: build() can run many times per second, so it must only describe UI from current state and do no side effects or heavy work.
body: Use when writing or editing a widget's build method. Do not start network calls, mutate state, create controllers, or run expensive computation inside build, which the framework may call on every frame. Actions: treat build as a pure function from state to widgets, move side effects to lifecycle methods (initState/didChangeDependencies) or event handlers, precompute heavy values, and mark unchanging subtrees const so they are not rebuilt. Validation signals include a widget test confirming the UI reflects state and a frame timing check showing build stays within budget. Risk boundary: side effects in build fire repeatedly and unpredictably (duplicate requests, leaked controllers), and heavy work there drops frames; a build that is not idempotent is a defect.
